safety_warnings: This plan is not a guarantee of recovery.
Do not try to bypass MFA, guess passwords, phish, or access accounts without authorization.
hardening_steps: Generate and safely store new backup codes.
Add a passkey where the service supports it.
Update recovery email and phone number.
Store recovery details in a trusted password manager.
Document business admin access and recovery contacts.

lost_mfa_device.support_summary: I lost access to my authenticator app and need to use the official recovery flow.
checklist: Try backup codes before any other recovery path.
Check whether a trusted device is still signed in.
Use the official account recovery page for the service.
Prepare identity, billing, device, or account ownership evidence.
Avoid repeated random attempts that can increase lockout risk.
evidence: Recovery email or recovery phone still under your control.

changed_phone_number.support_summary: My recovery phone number changed, and I need to recover access through official alternatives.
checklist: Try recovery email before requesting manual review.
Update the phone number only after access is restored.
Prepare proof that the account and old phone number belonged to you.
evidence: Recovery email still under your control.
Old and new phone number context.

suspicious_activity_lock.support_summary: My account was locked after suspicious activity, and I need to prove ownership safely.
checklist: Stop repeated login attempts that may extend the lockout.
Use the official locked-account or account recovery flow.
Review recent sign-in activity if any trusted device is still logged in.
Prepare ownership evidence before contacting support.
After recovery, change the password and revoke suspicious sessions.
evidence: Recent sign-in attempts you do not recognize.
